description: Poshitha Gamini Wanaguru Vs. Bandaranayake Mudiyanselage Shyamika Nayomi Bandaranayake and Another before Hon. K. M. S. DISSANAYAKE, J
parties: Poshitha Gamini Wanaguru Vs. Bandaranayake Mudiyanselage Shyamika Nayomi Bandaranayake and Another
judgement_by: Hon. K. M. S. DISSANAYAKE, J
keywords: Having failed to exercise the right of appeal to the Supreme Court in terms of section 14(2) of the Maintenance Act, to be read with sections 9(a) and 10 of the Act and Articles 154P(3)(b) and 138 of the Constitution, could the Petitioner invoke the extra-ordinary revisionary jurisdiction of this Court vested in it under Article 138 of the Constitution to revise and set aside an order made by a High Court of the Province in the exercise of the appellate jurisdiction vested in it by section 14(1) of the Maintenance Act, to be read with sections 4, 5 and 6 of the High Court of the Provinces (Special Provisions) Act, No. 19 of 1990 and Articles 154P(3)(b) and 138 of the Constitution in view of section 14(2) of the Maintenance Act to be read with sections 9(a) and 10 of the High Court of the Provinces (Special Provisions) Act, No. 19 of 1990.
legistation: Article 138, Article 154P(3)(b) of the Constitution of the Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka, Sections 3, 9(a) and 10 of the High Court of the Provinces (Special Provisions) Act, No. 19 of 1990, sections 14(1) and 14(2) of Maintenance Act No. 37 of 1999.
